assertions.expectation: Explains that Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for standard channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly states '24-month support vs 14-month on Regular' in the benefits section

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended channel has additional cost (it's not free)
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Higher licensing costs (requires GKE Enterprise)' in the limitations section

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended channel gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer
assertions.reason: Response doesn't explain that Extended gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them supported longer - it only mentions slower feature delivery

assertions.expectation: Warns that staying on older versions longer means missing new features and accumulating technical debt
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Newer features arrive later' and 'Less flexibility if you need cutting-edge features' and warns about 'slower feature adoption pace'

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel to Extended
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact gcloud command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --release-channel=extended --zone=ZONE'

assertions.expectation: Advises checking that the current version (1.31) is available on Extended before switching
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Extended channel currently supports 1.28.x and 1.29.x' and 'You'll need to downgrade from 1.31 since Extended doesn't support it yet'

assertions.expectation: Recommends Extended for environments with compliance requirements or slow internal change processes
assertions.reason: Response recommends Extended for 'production workloads requiring stability' and mentions it's 'Better for production clusters' with longer planning time

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended doesn't mean you can skip upgrades forever — you still must upgrade within 24 months
assertions.reason: Response doesn't explicitly mention that upgrades are still required within the 24-month window - it focuses on the longer support period but doesn't clarify upgrade obligations

assertions.expectation: Notes that minor version upgrades on Extended are NOT automated (except at EoS) — customers must plan and initiate them manually
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention that minor version upgrades are manual on Extended channel - it only mentions 'quarterly minor versions' and planning upgrades but doesn't specify the manual nature
